title: The two advisory surfaces, and the PreCompact additivity row they change
action: Per D4/D5/D9/D10/D11/D23/D24. bee-session-close.mjs on PreCompact: append the precompact record, render the D9 survival warning when cell_compact_count >= 2, and emit the anchor nudge with force:true bypassing dedup (matching maybeCaptureQueueNudge's force at :412-414). All three go through emitHookOutput as a systemMessage and NEVER encodeBlock — the B2/R14 contract at :234-238. bee-prompt-context.mjs: the deduped anchor nudge via shouldInject/markInjected, key 'anchor-missing-nudge', hash <sessionId>:<feature>:<cell>. THIS CELL MUST SPLIT AN EXISTING ASSERTION (D23), and it is the reason this cell owns hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: the PreCompact additivity row at :2692-2712 asserts the with-anchor message equals the anchor block plus the no-anchor control's EXACT bytes. The D10 nudge fires precisely when readIntent is null — true in the control fixture (preControl, :2656) and false in preRoot (:2657) — and buildFixture (:240-259) writes phase 'swarming' with approved_gates.execution true, so D10's predicate is satisfied in the control. The control therefore gains a nudge line the with-anchor message lacks, and the row goes red BY DESIGN. Split it the way D19 made unnecessary for SessionStart: keep an additivity assertion over the portion both messages share, and add an explicit assertion that the no-anchor control carries the nudge while the with-anchor message does not. Do not weaken the row to a substring match. Then the D24 regen chain: mirror both hooks, render plugin trees, self-onboard, release_manifest --write. Author scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs: PreCompact output contains no decision:'block' under any input; the warning appears on a cell's second compaction and not its first; the nudge fires on PreCompact even against a freshly-marked dedup cache; the nudge is suppressed on a second UserPromptSubmit within the interval; no nudge when an anchor exists or the phase is terminal. RED-FAILURE EVIDENCE IS REQUIRED AT CAP: record the suite failing against the un-wired hooks before wiring them.

truths: PreCompact output carries no turn-control verdict of any kind, asserted against the emitted payload.
The survival warning fires on a cell's second compaction and not its first.
The PreCompact nudge survives a freshly-marked dedup cache; the UserPromptSubmit nudge does not.

The split PreCompact row still asserts additivity over the shared portion and additionally asserts the nudge's presence in the control and absence in the with-anchor message — it is not weakened to a substring match.
The recorded red output against the un-wired hooks is attached as cap evidence.
release_manifest --check is green at cap time.
artifacts: Both wired hooks, their mirrors, the split PreCompact row, scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs, refreshed manifest
prohibitions: Never let the 30-minute dedup swallow the PreCompact nudge.
Never weaken the PreCompact additivity row to a substring or regex match to make it pass.
Never touch the SessionStart rows at :2740-2780, :2427-2455 or :2854-2883.

verify: node scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Both advisory surfaces wired: PreCompact appends the precompact record, renders the D9 survival warning off it, and forces the D10 anchor nudge past the dedup cache without marking it; UserPromptSubmit emits the same nudge deduped on anchor-missing-nudge. New 7-row scripts/test_compaction_advisories.mjs drives both real hooks; the D23 PreCompact additivity row split into two byte-exact rows (identical tails below the two leads + nudge present/absent), never weakened to a substring.

checks.evidence: test_hook_contracts.mjs:2731-2737 uses raw preTail === preControlTail; :2765-2769 uses preControlLead === preNudgeExpected against bytes from the fixture own vendored compaction.mjs. Judge mutations on a throwaway copy: MUT1 (delete the PreCompact nudge push) -> BOTH rows FAIL; MUT2 (one-word paraphrase VERBATIM->verbatim) -> row b FAIL; MUT3 (extra part on the anchor path only) -> row a FAIL. No substring or regex used for either equality.

test_hook_contracts.mjs:2736 asserts the tail carries the hive-door warning. MUT4 (suppress that warning so both tails go empty) -> row a FAIL with controlTail= withTail=null. The non-vacuity guard bites.

Judge reproduced the red rather than trusting the trace: pre-split test_hook_contracts.mjs from 8743c28~1 run against post-wiring hooks gives exactly one failure, precompact-no-anchor-output-is-untouched-anchor-is-additive, 193 rows 1 failing — matching the recorded evidence verbatim. Post-split is 194 rows: net +1, nothing removed.

bee-session-close.mjs:318-325 gates shouldInject AND markInjected behind if(!force). test_compaction_advisories.mjs:344-363 runs the real PreCompact hook, asserts shouldInject still true, then runs the real UserPromptSubmit hook and asserts the nudge still fires. MUT5 (move markInjected outside the guard) -> that row FAIL, 6 passed 1 failed.

Sole encodeBlock call site is bee-session-close.mjs:472, guarded by maybeBypassBlock whose first statement is if(!ctx || ctx.event !== Stop) return null (:385-387). adapter.mjs:363-372 emitHookOutput can only emit encodeAdvisory or plain text — no decision field on that path. A missing hook_event_name yields ctx.event === empty string which also fails the Stop guard. Unreachable under any input.

test_compaction_advisories.mjs:269-276 asserts the same bypass fixture DOES emit decision:block on Stop. MUT7 (short-circuit maybeBypassBlock to return null) -> that row FAIL with the message the bypass fixture must block on Stop, otherwise the PreCompact row proves nothing. The row discriminates the event, not the fixture.

bee-session-close.mjs:311-315 calls survivalWarning on appendCompactionRecord RETURN VALUE, never a re-read. compaction.mjs:277-290 makes the count self-inclusive with threshold 2 at :66, so a stale pre-append read would give 1 on the second compaction and null the warning, reddening test_compaction_advisories.mjs:301-306. The row discriminates the exact off-by-one by construction.
git show 8743c28 on that file returns exactly two hunks: the node:url import and @@ -2689,25 +2689,91 @@. Lines 2427-2455, 2740-2780 and 2854-2883 all sit outside it.

Per-hook placement is right for a reason the worker did NOT give: bee.mjs:2429 calls anchorMissing inside the read-only compact-check sweep, and both suites call it as a side-effect-free oracle. Folding markInjected into compaction.mjs would make a reporting command silently consume the 30-minute nudge budget — a direct violation of D13 reports-never-repairs — and would make the tests mutate the cache they measure. Not a consolidation candidate.
